step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture is reconstructed based on the world model, whiteboard connections, and video transcript. The user accesses the system through Route 53 which routes traffic via ALB to EKS. Microservice jobs are run on Spot instances. To ensure reliability against spot terminations, job execution state events are published to SNS, which fans out to SQS. Newly spawned/replaced jobs can read unfinished state from SQS to resume execution.

graph.name: OLX Autos Internal Developer Platform with EKS and Spot Instances
graph.notes: OLX Autos uses an internal developer platform on Amazon EKS running 100% on Spot Instances to create replica environments. They utilize AWS Node Termination Handler, SNS, and SQS to track microservice deployment job states and gracefully recover from spot interruptions.

nodes.name: USER
nodes.notes: Represents the developer or end-user of the platform requesting environment creation through the Orion portal.

nodes.name: ROUTE 53
nodes.notes: Used for DNS resolution of the request to the Orion portal.

nodes.name: ALB
nodes.notes: Application Load Balancer distributing incoming traffic to EKS resources.

nodes.name: EKS
nodes.notes: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service managing and orchestrating containerized microservices.

nodes.name: SPOT
nodes.notes: 100% Spot Instances running the actual workloads, with node termination handlers deployed.

nodes.name: SNS
nodes.notes: Simple Notification Service to publish state events when microservice provisioning jobs are triggered.

nodes.name: SQS
nodes.notes: Simple Queue Service queue to hold state messages for newly spun up jobs to recover state.

nodes.name: Microservices Jobs (m.s. 1, m.s. 2, m.s. 3)
nodes.notes: Kubernetes jobs representing individual microservice deployments.

edges.notes: User makes a request to the Orion portal resolved by Route 53
DNS resolves and sends traffic to the ALB
ALB routes requests to the Kubernetes pod orchestrator
ALB directs traffic to pods hosted on spot instances
EKS triggers Kubernetes jobs to deploy specific microservices
Kubernetes jobs execute and deploy on top of Spot instances
The EKS control plane / jobs send execution state notifications to SNS
SNS fans out state messages to SQS
Replaced jobs read unfinished job states from SQS to resume execution gracefully
